titleStmt.title: A Transcription of John in P66
P66
The New Testament
The Gospel according to John
john

respStmt.name: the International Greek New Testament Project

titleStmt.funder: The Arts and Humanities Research Council

sponsor.name: The Institute for Textual Scholarship and Electronic Editing

publisher.name: The Institute for Textual Scholarship and Electronic Editing
University of Birmingham

availability.p: Licensed under a Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International License (CC BY 4.0): available for re-use provided attribution is made to the original creators (the IGNTP and any named participants) and an indication is given of any changes made. See further .

projectDesc.p: This transcription was made by the International Greek New Testament Project in its work towards the Editio Critica Maior of John between 2000 and 2010.

editorialDecl.p: The initial transcription file was a plain text file for use with COLLATE, later converted to XML. Further information about the procedures followed may be found in the electronic edition of the majuscules at
The plain text file was converted to XML by Catherine Smith using the project's Python conversion scripts 1.0.
The XML specifications used are version 1.6 of the IGNTP Guidelines available at

revisionDesc.change: corrections at 2.11 and 4.48 changed by David Parker.
lac rest verse at 16.6 added by David Parker.
lac rest line at 16.2 removed by David Parker.
C* at 17.14 changed by David Parker.
Supplied text at 16.11 changed by David Parker.
Supplied text at 19.29 changed by David Parker.
[να]ζ[ω]ρ[19-23] changed to [να]ζ[ω]ρ[αιον] rest of line lac at 18.5 by Rachel Kevern.
End of 16.7 verse marker moved by Rachel Kevern.
του τουτου changed to τουτου του at 15.19 by Rachel Kevern.
βα θυ changed to βαθυ in * 4.11 and [18-21]τω changed to [16-19] [αυ]τω at 18.31 by Rachel Kevern.
[ουν] changed to [συν] at 21.3 by Rachel Kevern.
C* ην added to δ[ε μαθητης εκε]ινο[ς ην γνωσ]τος at 18.15 by Rachel Kevern (was missing in previous edit).
C* δ[11]ινο[7]τος changed to δ[ε μαθητης εκε]ινο[ς ην γνωσ]τος at 18.15 by Rachel Kevern.
2-3 line gap changed to 2-3 char gap at 4.6 by Rachel Kevern.

Word break at 4.42 corrected and empty word tag at 18.1 deleted and apostrophe added to same verse, apostrophe put in pc tags at 8.29, apostrophe moved into preceding word tags at 8.54, 8.56 and 9.28 by Rachel Kevern.
Gap tags and uncertain spaces at the beginning of 16.29 and uncertain spaces on P154 changed, apostrophe moved to preceding word at 4.5 and 15.19 by Rachel Kevern.
Word break at 3.31 corrected by Rachel Kevern.
Empty word tag at 13.23 removed by Rachel Kevern.
' moved inside each word of correction in 9:7 by Catherine Smith
Rest of verse lac tags deleted from 16.11 by Rachel Kevern.
[4-5] changed back to [ειδως] at 18.4 and rest of verse lac tags added to 16.10, 16.11 and 16.12 by Rachel Kevern.
Rest of verse lac tags and rest of line lac tags added to 20.20, 22, 23, 25 and 26 by by Rachel Kevern.
Rest of verse lac tag replaced by [ου] at 17.20 by Rachel Kevern.
Lac tags improved at 16.23 and 16.24 by Rachel Kevern.

υ[μων] changed back to υ[μιν] at 16.6, [6] changed back to [λαλεις] and λ[5] changed back to λ[εγεις] at 16.29, [7] changed back to [εγνωκαν] at 17.7, word element added to gap element at 18.4 by Rachel Kevern.

Some supplied text replaced by gap tag at 16.19, gap tag added at 17.20, [5-6] replaced by [ην ο το] at 19.20, ειπ[ε] changed to ειπ[εν] at 19.21, κ[4]ει[νος] changed to κ[αι εκ]ει[νος] at 19.35 and [ανεβ]ησ[αν] changed to [ενεβ]ησ[αν] at 21.3 by Rachel Kevern.
κα[...] [1-2]ν changed to κα[ι] [εα]ν at 15.4 and lac elements added 16.11 and 16.12 by Rachel Kevern.
Line breaks removed from segs by Catherine Smith.
[11-13]σαται changed to [11-13] [part word lacuna]σαται at 16.24, [6]λλα changed to [εχητε α]λλα at 16.33 and [6]παρας changed to [6] [part word lacuna]παρας at 17.1 by Rachel Kevern.
ει[3] changed to εις το and [2-3]τα changed to [αυ]τα at 15.6; υμ[1]ς changed to υμ[α]ς at 15.21 and 'rest of verse' gap tag added at 15.2 by Rachel Kevern.
Apostrophe after app tags removed at 7.31 by Rachel Kevern.
Random apostrophes after app tags removed at 13.23 and 18.1 by Rachel Kevern.
Correction at 13.7 corrected and two new correctors added to listWit by Rachel Kevern.
Word breaks at 18.5, 18.8 and 18.18 corrected. [επεσα] changed to [επεσαν] at 18.6. [ειδως] changed to [4-5] at 18.4. εμισει *σε C om changed to *εμισεισε C εμισει at 15.18. All by Rachel Kevern.

[εδωκας] changed to [6-7] and [δεδ]ωκας changed to [2-3]ωκας at 17.8.[δεδω]κας changed to [3-4]κας at 17.9. [πιστευο]ντων changed to [7-8]ντων at 17.20. [δεδωκας] changed to [6-7] at 17.24. All by Rachel Kevern.
[λαλεις] changed to [6], [λεγεις] changed to [6] at 16.29 and [εγνωκαν] changed to [7] at 17.7 by Rachel Kevern.
υ[μιν] changed to υ[μων] at 16.6, ημ[5]της changed to ημ[ερα] [αυ]της at 16.21 and word break corrected at 16.19 by Rachel Kevern.
ου κυ changed to ουκυ at 12.39 by Rachel Kevern.
Correction to tagging of end of 21.9 by David Parker.
Word breaks in 7.45, 8.43, 46, 11.54, 13.37, 14.19 corrected by David Parker.
Word breaks corrected in 2.11, 3.15, 9.25, 12,5, 18.25, 19.20, 21.2, 21.5 by David Parker.
Ghost page removed by Catherine Smith.
Adjusted to XML specifications 1.6 (apart from handling of ghost pages), including witnessEnd marking, by Hugh Houghton.
line lacs adding at beginning and end of 6 lines P115C1L11-P115C1L16
Koppa character fixed by Hugh Houghton.
Converted from plain text by Catherine Smith using Python conversion scripts 1.0 with manual adjustments.

body.note: Where text is obscured by an overlying piece of papyrus, we follow Martin who was able to view the obscured text, and sο record it as extant. This affects pages 2, 4, 15, 17, 27, 60, 62, 63, 64, 73, 75, 77, 114

ab.note: There may or may not be an erased letter between μ and α
There are four pages missing after this page. V35 begins on P39
The scribe began to write χ and changed it to κ.
There are traces of ink on this line representing parts of some letters but their identification is not secure.
What may be part of a τ is visible and it is most likely that the cοrrectοr wrοte τη
The correction symbol after μοι indicates that missing text was supplied in the bοttοm margin
Ιt is pοssible that there is a corrector's insertion symbol here but not certain
All that survives of this page is traces of a character followed by an epsilon.
There are traces of ink on this line representing parts of some letters but their identification is not secure.
